# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.132.0/http/server.ts";
const port = 8000; // default to 8000 if not provided in environment variables or query parameters, e.g., Deno Deploy's edge runtime will set this automatically for us by the serverless framework when running on it (DenoDeploy)
import { readConfig } from "./config/index.ts"; // assuming config module is exporting a function to get configuration data 
const app = serve(async (req: Request): Promise<Response> => {
    const envData = await Deno.readTextFile("/.env");//assuming .deno_deploy file contains environment variables for the application, read it here and use in your code as needed e.g., process.env 
     // parse config from query parameters or request headers if not provided by denodo deploy framework (DenoDeploy)  
    const userConfig = await Deno.readTextFile("/.user_config");//assuming .deno_deploy file contains configuration data for the application, read it here and use in your code as needed e.g., process.env 
     // merge envData with default config (if provided) to get final userConfig  
    const mergedUserConfig = {...defaultConfig,...userConfig};//assuming there is a 'default_config' object that contains the defaults for all configurations, read it here and use in your code as needed e.g., process.env 
     // load modules based on configuration using dynamic import()  
    const modulePaths = mergedUserConfig?.modulepaths || [];//assuming there is a 'user_config' object that contains the paths of all loaded custom plugins, read it here and use in your code as needed e.g., process.env 
     for (const path of modulePaths) {   // loop over each imported plugin   
        const mod = await import(path);//load dynamic modules based on user configuration using Deno's built-in `import()` function and async/await to handle errors gracefully, read it here as needed e.g., process the module data or throw an error if necessary  
         // do something with mod (e.g., use its functions)    } 
});//end of serve handler for http requests on given port number     });      };       start server and listen to incoming connections at specified ports, read it here as needed e.g., process the configuration data or throw an error if necessary   })(); // end main function call in async/await style (starting Deno Deploy's edge runtime)
